data.title: Correct gascity topology + tame dolt CPU (rigs/cities split-brain, order churn, telemetry)
data.description: Managed dolt server (PID 1606) pegged ~125% CPU from a firehose of short-lived bd connections (~34-160 conn/s) generated by gc supervisor running a large per-rig order set with create/close wisp tracking beads, across 3 rigs (hq, factoryskills, lenny) each carrying a full pack agent set (~20 agents). factoryskills is triple-mounted: rig DB on managed server 1606, its own standalone-city dolt server (16498), and a stray rig-local server (78724 on :62895) — flagged as zombies/drift by gc dolt health. dolt send-metrics telemetry also respawning ~40% CPU (not disabled). Goal: research official gc recommended topology, then correct structure + cool the server. User authorized Tier 1+2+3 (everything idle until fixed).
data.design:
DIFF RESULT (factoryskills managed:34947 vs rig-local:62895) — NOT clean, do NOT discard rig-local:
- Managed: 274 issues, newest issue update 01:40 UTC. Rig-local: 215 issues, newest 02:26 UTC (rig-local has the MORE RECENT factoryskills work — agents were effectively writing there late, despite mirror=34947).
- 3 IDs unique to rig-local: factoryskills-3uon (open, 02:26, 'gc sling --on does not inject source bead'), factoryskills-g7oi (open, 01:42, 'PR opened notification...'), factoryskills-wmhx (closed, 'test' throwaway).
- 62 IDs unique to managed; 212 shared.
- 6 shared issues newer in rig-local (edits only in rig-local): -14qo,-69l,-dw7,-hydd,-luio,-zwx (several at 02:26).
=> Reconciliation required before retiring rig-local: migrate the 2 real unique open issues + 6 newer edits from rig-local into managed (or pick rig-local as canonical and backfill the 62). Decide canonical store first. CSVs saved /tmp/fs_managed.csv, /tmp/fs_riglocal.csv.

TOPOLOGY CORRECTION (per user + docs.gascityhall.com/concepts/architecture-overview#rig): rigs do NOT have to live under <city-root>/rigs/ — external paths are allowed. So factoryskills/lenny living at ~/development/com.nicospencer/* is NOT a violation. Drop that item. The real defects remain: (a) factoryskills carrying its own city scaffold + 2 extra dolt servers (split-brain), (b) order-churn idle overhead. Authoritative sources at ~/development/com.gastownhall/.
data.notes: Cross-session coordination: left an actionable comment on factoryskills-m2sw (the rig-aware gc_sling dispatch tier, in the 'fs delegates to gc' epic factoryskills-hrfy). m2sw's spec emits 'gc sling <rig>/fspack.<seat>' — same fspack-vs-pack hardcode class as 3n0x. Comment warns: derive the seat (gc agent list / import key), don't hardcode fspack; note internal/packguard (#220) only scans pack/orders/*.toml so it won't catch a hardcoded fspack in fs dispatch CODE — extend the guard over the dispatch layer. The two/three work modes (gc-dispatch / fs --inline / pure hand-work) are being codified by epic hrfy: m2sw=via tiers, zp84=/discover presents gc-recommended-vs-inline, r2nk=collapse to 3 skills (discover/factory/factoryskills). Modes doc should anchor to zp84.
